message:
feat: add Vibe and Factory providers with flexible JSONL parsing

Vibe: flexible field extraction (role/speaker, content/text, 5 timestamp
field names), session root at ~/.vibe/logs/session/*/messages.jsonl.

Factory: typed JSONL entries (session_start + message), workspace slug
encoding, settings file support for model info, session root at
~/.factory/sessions/{workspace-slug}/{uuid}.jsonl.

Also adds missing env overrides (CHATGPT_HOME, CLAWDBOT_HOME, VIBE_HOME,
FACTORY_HOME) to CLI e2e test harness to prevent real session leakage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1kg.2","title":"Implement resume command output and UX","description":"Implement the user-facing output for `casr <target> resume <session-id>`. This is what the user sees when they run the tool.

Success output example:

  ✓ Found session 019c3eae-94c3-7d73-9b2a-9edb18f1563b
    Source: Codex (codex)
    Workspace: /data/projects/myapp
    Messages: 47 (23 user, 24 assistant)
    Duration: 2h 15m

  ✓ Converted to Claude Code format
    Written to: ~/.claude/projects/abc123/new-session-id.jsonl
    Verified: read-back check passed ✓

  Resume with:
    claude --resume new-session-id

Backup notification (when `--force` was used and an existing session was backed up):

  ⚠ Existing session backed up to ~/.claude/projects/abc123/old-session-id.jsonl.bak

Error output examples:

  ✗ Session ID 'bad-id' not found
    Searched: Claude Code (roots: 2, files: 23), Codex (roots: 1, files: 156), Gemini (roots: 1, files: 12)
    Tip: Run `casr list` to see available sessions
    Tip: Use `--source <provider>` if you know the origin

  ✗ Target provider not writable
    Provider: Claude Code
    Evidence: ~/.claude/projects is read-only
    Tip: Fix permissions or set `CLAUDE_HOME` to a writable location

  ✗ Session already exists in target
    Existing: ~/.claude/projects/abc123/existing-session-id.jsonl (47 messages, 2h ago)
    Use `--force` to overwrite (creates .bak), or resume existing:
      claude --resume existing-session-id

  ✗ Write verification failed (this is a bug in casr)
    The written file could not be read back. Please report this with `--trace --json` output.

UX requirements:
- Use `colored` crate for colors (TTY-aware).
- Show progress for large sessions.
- In `--json` mode, output structured JSON instead of human text.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Success output includes: source provider, workspace, message counts, written paths, and resume command.
- Warnings (validation warnings, binary-missing warnings) are visible in human output and present in `--json` result.
- Errors are actionable and include remediation hints.
- Output formatting is deterministic enough for e2e tests (bd-24z.5/bd-24z.6).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:42:00.316169403Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T13:15:43.902957521Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T13:15:43.902863214Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1kg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:13.674318857Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1kg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:50.623081923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:04:16.265126132Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1kg.3","title":"Implement list and info subcommands","description":"Implement the `casr list` and `casr info <session-id>` subcommands.

## `casr list`

Scan all readable providers and display a table of sessions:

  Provider     Session ID                              Workspace                    Date          Messages
  claude-code  40f2cb68-fed7-4cee-83de-2b63ba9b7813   /data/projects/myapp         2026-02-07    47
  codex        019c3eae-94c3-7d73-9b2a-9edb18f1563b   /data/projects/other         2026-02-06    156
  gemini       3bdd5aeb-a260-4e20-94a3-3fa5a0b8f7b8   /data/projects/webapp        2026-02-05    247

Optional filters:
- `--provider <slug>` (only one provider)
- `--workspace <path>` (filter by workspace)
- `--limit <n>`
- `--sort <field>` (date/messages/provider)

## `casr info <session-id>`

Display details without converting:

  Session: 019c3eae-94c3-7d73-9b2a-9edb18f1563b
  Provider: Codex
  Workspace: /data/projects/other
  Started: 2026-02-06 14:30:00 UTC
  Ended: 2026-02-06 16:45:00 UTC
  Duration: 2h 15m
  Messages: 156 (78 user, 78 assistant)
  Model: o3-pro
  Source: ~/.codex/sessions/2026/02/06/rollout-1.jsonl

  First message: "Help me implement the auth module..."
  Last message: "All tests passing. Ready to deploy."

In `--json` mode, output structured JSON. These commands read from disk without converting.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `list` and `info` operate on providers with readable roots (binary not required).
- `list` supports filtering/sorting/limit as documented.
- `info` uses bd-3my.2 to resolve session ids and surfaces `AmbiguousSessionId` when applicable.
- `--json` output is schema-tested (bd-24z.11).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:42:12.866806438Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T13:15:43.981944551Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T13:15:43.981859812Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1kg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:15.105850276Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1kg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:50.768209616Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3my.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:50.838055419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1kg.4","title":"Add shell completion generation (bash/zsh/fish)","description":"Use `clap_complete` to generate shell completions for bash, zsh, and fish. Add a `casr completions <shell>` subcommand that writes completion scripts to stdout.

Usage example:

```bash
eval "$(casr completions bash)"
```

Implementation notes:
- `clap_complete` integrates with clap derive: add dependency and generator wiring.
- Completions must include all subcommands, flags, and arguments.
- Provider aliases (`cc`, `cod`, `gmi`) must be offered as completions for `<target>`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `casr completions bash|zsh|fish` prints valid completion scripts.
- Completion scripts include all global flags and subcommands.
- Provider alias completions are correct and tested in a CLI integration test.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:05:21.522469158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T13:15:44.060971045Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T13:15:44.060905062Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1kg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:14.498347652Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1kg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:05:25.201245070Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1mp","title":"Implement Factory provider adapter (JSONL with session metadata and workspace slugs)","description":"PORT Factory provider from CASS: /dp/coding_agent_session_search/src/connectors/factory.rs

WHAT IS FACTORY: Factory.ai, an AI coding assistant that stores sessions as structured JSONL.

SESSION FORMAT:
  Location: ~/.factory/sessions/{workspace-path-slug}/{session-uuid}.jsonl
  Env override: FACTORY_HOME
  Workspace slug encoding: Path separators replaced with dashes, e.g. /Users/alice/Dev/myproject → -Users-alice-Dev-myproject
  Optional settings: {session-uuid}.settings.json (contains model info)

  First JSONL line (session header):
    {"type":"session_start","id":"uuid","title":"...","owner":"...","cwd":"/path","timestamp":"..."}
  Subsequent lines (messages):
    {"type":"message","timestamp":"...","message":{"role":"user|assistant","content":"..."}}

KEY DISTINGUISHING FEATURES:
  1. WORKSPACE SLUG ENCODING: Directory names encode the workspace path. Must decode slug→path for workspace field.
  2. SESSION METADATA HEADER: First line is type=session_start with id, title, cwd, timestamp.
  3. WRAPPED MESSAGES: Messages have outer type="message" wrapper with inner "message" object.
  4. SETTINGS FILE: Optional .settings.json file alongside session file contains model info.

PROVIDER TRAIT IMPLEMENTATION:
  - slug: "factory"
  - cli_alias: "fct"
  - name: "Factory"
  - detect(): Check for ~/.factory/sessions/ directory (or FACTORY_HOME)
  - session_roots(): Return [~/.factory/sessions/]
  - owns_session(id): Walk session roots looking for {id}.jsonl files
  - read_session(path):
    1. Parse first line as session_start → extract session_id, title, cwd (workspace), timestamp
    2. Parse remaining lines as type=message → unwrap inner message object → normalize role/content/timestamp
    3. Optionally read .settings.json for model_name
  - write_session(session, opts):
    1. Compute workspace slug from session.workspace
    2. Write session_start header line
    3. Write message lines with type=message wrapper
    4. Optionally write .settings.json with model info
    5. Use atomic_write
  - resume_command(id): "factory --resume {id}" (best guess)

CONSIDERATIONS:
  - Workspace slug decode: replace leading dash with /, then all remaining dashes with /. Edge case: dashes IN path components. CASS uses a heuristic — study it carefully.
  - The wrapped message format (type="message" + inner "message" object) is similar to OpenClaw/Pi-Agent
  - Session metadata (title, owner) is richer than ClawdBot/Vibe
  - Settings file is optional — don't fail if missing

REFERENCE: CASS connector is ~250 lines. This is medium complexity. Follow Codex pattern for JSONL with metadata header.

FILES TO CREATE/MODIFY:
  - CREATE: src/providers/factory.rs
  - MODIFY: src/providers/mod.rs (add pub mod factory)
  - MODIFY: src/discovery.rs (add to default_registry, add JSONL signature check for "session_start" type)
  - MODIFY: src/main.rs (update provider list in docs)
  - MODIFY: tests/json_contract_test.rs (update count + alias + FACTORY_HOME env)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:05:48.225854201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T03:11:21.474766446Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-1mp","title":"Implement Factory provider adapter (JSONL with session metadata and workspace slugs)","description":"PORT Factory provider from CASS: /dp/coding_agent_session_search/src/connectors/factory.rs

WHAT IS FACTORY: Factory.ai, an AI coding assistant that stores sessions as structured JSONL.

SESSION FORMAT:
  Location: ~/.factory/sessions/{workspace-path-slug}/{session-uuid}.jsonl
  Env override: FACTORY_HOME
  Workspace slug encoding: Path separators replaced with dashes, e.g. /Users/alice/Dev/myproject → -Users-alice-Dev-myproject
  Optional settings: {session-uuid}.settings.json (contains model info)

  First JSONL line (session header):
    {"type":"session_start","id":"uuid","title":"...","owner":"...","cwd":"/path","timestamp":"..."}
  Subsequent lines (messages):
    {"type":"message","timestamp":"...","message":{"role":"user|assistant","content":"..."}}

KEY DISTINGUISHING FEATURES:
  1. WORKSPACE SLUG ENCODING: Directory names encode the workspace path. Must decode slug→path for workspace field.
  2. SESSION METADATA HEADER: First line is type=session_start with id, title, cwd, timestamp.
  3. WRAPPED MESSAGES: Messages have outer type="message" wrapper with inner "message" object.
  4. SETTINGS FILE: Optional .settings.json file alongside session file contains model info.

PROVIDER TRAIT IMPLEMENTATION:
  - slug: "factory"
  - cli_alias: "fct"
  - name: "Factory"

  - session_roots(): Return [~/.factory/sessions/]
  - owns_session(id): Walk session roots looking for {id}.jsonl files

    1. Parse first line as session_start → extract session_id, title, cwd (workspace), timestamp
    2. Parse remaining lines as type=message → unwrap inner message object → normalize role/content/timestamp
    3. Optionally read .settings.json for model_name
  - write_session(session, opts):

    5. Use atomic_write
  - resume_command(id): "factory --resume {id}" (best guess)

CONSIDERATIONS:
  - Workspace slug decode: replace leading dash with /, then all remaining dashes with /. Edge case: dashes IN path components. CASS uses a heuristic — study it carefully.
  - The wrapped message format (type="message" + inner "message" object) is similar to OpenClaw/Pi-Agent
  - Session metadata (title, owner) is richer than ClawdBot/Vibe
  - Settings file is optional — don't fail if missing

REFERENCE: CASS connector is ~250 lines. This is medium complexity. Follow Codex pattern for JSONL with metadata header.

FILES TO CREATE/MODIFY:
  - CREATE: src/providers/factory.rs
  - MODIFY: src/providers/mod.rs (add pub mod factory)
  - MODIFY: src/discovery.rs (add to default_registry, add JSONL signature check for "session_start" type)

  - MODIFY: tests/json_contract_test.rs (update count + alias + FACTORY_HOME env)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"CrimsonCanyon","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:05:48.225854201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T03:16:51.933647244Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1od","title":"Improve --source <path> resolution when path is outside provider roots","description":"Currently SourceHint::Path resolution falls back to the first installed provider when the path is not under any provider session root, which can mis-parse orphaned/moved session files. Instead, infer the owning provider via lightweight file signature heuristics and/or probing installed providers to select the best match deterministically.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:43:09.312330539Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T01:48:32.850343566Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T01:48:32.850323148Z","close_reason":"Improved SourceHint::Path resolution: if path not under any provider root, infer provider via lightweight file signature (json/jsonl/vscdb) or probe providers and pick most plausible parser; updated discovery unit test; cargo fmt/clippy/test pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","discovery"]}
 {"id":"bd-1rr","title":"Implement ChatGPT provider adapter (port from CASS)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:54:43.685465801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T03:02:53.162947226Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T03:02:53.162922730Z","close_reason":"ChatGPT provider implemented: reader (mapping + simple messages), writer (mapping format), detection, owns_session, 31 tests, all 508 tests pass, clippy clean","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-20x","title":"EPIC: Provider Readers (Import from Native Format)","description":"Implement readers that parse each provider's native session format into the canonical IR. Each reader takes a session file path and produces a `CanonicalSession`.

Scope:
- Claude Code (`~/.claude/projects/*.jsonl/.json/.claude`)
- Codex (`~/.codex/sessions/**/rollout-*.jsonl` + legacy JSON)
- Gemini CLI (`~/.gemini/tmp/<hash>/chats/session-*.json`)

Port and adapt connector parsing logic from CASS (`/dp/coding_agent_session_search/src/connectors/`) for behavior parity.

Critical architecture constraint:
- casr MUST vendor/copy/adapt relevant CASS logic directly into casr source.
- casr MUST NOT depend on CASS as an external runtime/library dependency.

Reader acceptance requirements:
1. Handle all known format variants (JSONL vs JSON, string vs block-array content, mixed timestamp formats).
2. Preserve provider-specific non-canonical fields in `session.metadata` and `message.extra`.
3. Be permissive on malformed lines/events (skip with diagnostics where safe) and fail only on unrecoverable session corruption.
4. Produce deterministic output ordering and stable parsing behavior for tests.
5. Emit trace-level diagnostics at key decisions (role mapping, timestamp fallback path, workspace inference path).

This epic is the import half of the conversion pipeline.

## Success Criteria

- Core readers pass fixture tests (synthetic + redacted-real) and parity suite.
- Readers are resilient to malformed inputs and produce actionable warnings.
- Reader outputs preserve high-value provider-specific raw fields in `metadata`/`extra` for writer fidelity.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:37:54.035149598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:58:34.791994780Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:58:34.791969082Z","close_reason":"Core readers complete (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI). bd-20x.4 (additional providers) is P3 future work, not blocking.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","readers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20x","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:04.615515810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:00:29.378282025Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -42,7 +42,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-24z.7","title":"Implement unit tests for canonical model helpers","description":"Write unit tests for the helper functions in `src/model.rs`: `flatten_content`, `parse_timestamp`, `normalize_role`, `truncate_title`, `reindex_messages`.

These helpers handle the messiest part of format conversion and must be exhaustively tested.

Tests for `flatten_content()`:
- string input -> returns the string
- array of `{type:"text", text:"..."}` -> concatenates all text blocks
- array of `{type:"input_text", text:"..."}` -> concatenates text blocks
- `{type:"tool_use", name:"Bash", input:{description:"run tests"}}` -> stable tool marker text
- mixed arrays containing text + tool_use + strings
- object without `type` but with `text`
- array of strings
- empty array / null / numbers / booleans
- nested arrays (if supported)

Tests for `parse_timestamp()`:
- ISO-8601 with Z and fractional
- ISO-8601 with timezone offsets
- epoch seconds vs millis heuristic (including numeric strings)
- float strings
- invalid/null/object/array inputs return None

Tests for `normalize_role()`:
- user/assistant/model/agent/tool/system mappings (case-insensitive)
- unknown roles map to Other(role)

Tests for `truncate_title()`:
- first line extraction
- trimming
- truncation with `...`
- empty/whitespace -> empty

Tests for `reindex_messages()`:
- gaps are removed
- empty vec no-op

Logging:
- Each test logs input and expected output.
- On failure, show raw serde_json::Value input alongside parsed result.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Helper tests cover edge cases that commonly appear in provider formats.
- Tests are deterministic and produce high-signal failure output.
- No helper panics on malformed inputs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:06:45.681532791Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T13:19:51.142657818Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T13:19:51.142590713Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","model","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24z.7","depends_on_id":"bd-24z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:11.446203512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24z.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:00:29.682215189Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24z.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:06:49.976646174Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-24z.8","title":"Implement unit tests for provider detection and session discovery","description":"Write unit tests for provider detection + session discovery (`ProviderRegistry` + bd-3my.2 resolver).

## ProviderRegistry Tests

1. `new()` creates registry with all 3 providers.
2. `find_by_slug("claude-code")` returns Some; unknown slug returns None.
3. `find_by_alias("cc"|"cod"|"gmi")` returns correct provider.
4. `detect_all()` returns a `ProviderStatus` for each provider with evidence-rich detection.
5. `providers_for_read()` includes providers with readable roots even if binaries are missing.
6. `providers_for_write()` includes providers with writable/creatable roots even if binaries are missing.

## Session Ownership Resolver Tests (bd-3my.2)

1. With a synthetic session file in a temp Claude Code directory, resolution finds (Claude, path).
2. With a synthetic session file in a temp Codex directory, resolution finds (Codex, path).
3. With a synthetic session file in a temp Gemini directory, resolution finds (Gemini, path).
4. Non-existent session ID returns `SessionNotFound` with provider/root/file-count diagnostics.
5. Ambiguous session ID present in multiple providers returns `AmbiguousSessionId` with all candidate providers/paths.
6. `--source <alias>` narrows search to one provider.
7. `--source <path>` bypasses discovery and resolves directly.

Use env var overrides (`CLAUDE_HOME`, `CODEX_HOME`, `GEMINI_HOME`) to redirect to temp dirs.

## Logging/Debug Requirements

- Log each directory scanned, each fast-path checked, and the final decision.
- On failure, show directory listings for temp roots and the resolver trace (decision path).

## Acceptance Criteria

- Tests cover unique match, no match, ambiguous match, alias hint, and path hint.
- Tests cover detection evidence and can_read/can_write behavior.
- Failures are debuggable from test output alone.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:07:01.074925884Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T16:09:15.219362854Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T16:09:15.219325654Z","close_reason":"Added dedicated ProviderRegistry/discovery unit suite (10 tests) covering slug/alias lookup, detection evidence, installed filtering, unique/not-found/ambiguous resolution, and alias/path hint behavior with temp files.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["discovery","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24z.8","depends_on_id":"bd-24z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:11.246807410Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24z.8","depends_on_id":"bd-24z.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:07:05.811582071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24z.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3my.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:07:05.656268912Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24z.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3my.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:07:05.733538517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-24z.9","title":"Implement unit tests for conversion pipeline with mock providers","description":"Write unit tests for the `ConversionPipeline` using mock providers. Since the pipeline is generic over the `Provider` trait, we can test it with in-memory mock providers that do not touch the filesystem.

## Mock Provider Harness

Create a `MockProvider` that implements `Provider`:
- `detect()` returns configurable `DetectionResult` (binary present/missing; roots readable/writable).
- `owns_session(id)` returns Some(path) for a predefined set.
- `read_session(path)` returns a hardcoded `CanonicalSession` or a controlled error.
- `write_session(session, opts)` records writes and returns `WrittenSession` with fake paths + optional backups.
- `resume_command(id)` returns a formatted string.

Also add a mock resolver hook or deterministic registry layout so bd-3my.2 behaviors (unique/ambiguous/not-found) can be exercised without touching disk.

## Pipeline Tests

Happy paths:
1. Convert from MockA to MockB succeeds; `write_session()` called with expected canonical session.
2. Dry run: `dry_run=true` does not call `write_session()`.
3. Same-provider shortcut: source==target returns early with resume command and no writes.

Discovery/error paths:
4. Session not found: returns `SessionNotFound` with diagnostics.
5. Ambiguous session id: returns `AmbiguousSessionId` and never reads/writes.
6. Target not writable: returns `ProviderUnavailable` (or equivalent) and never writes.
7. Target binary missing but writable: conversion succeeds and emits a WARNING in result.
8. Source hint by alias narrows provider search.
9. Source hint by path bypasses discovery.

Validation coverage (MUST cover ALL checks from bd-ikb.2):
10. Empty session -> `ValidationError`.
11. User-only -> `ValidationError`.
12. Assistant-only -> `ValidationError`.
13. No workspace -> warning (pipeline continues).
14. No timestamps -> warning.
15. Consecutive same-role messages -> warning.
16. Unmatched tool_result.call_id -> info (verbose/trace only).

Verify/rollback:
17. Verify-after-write success path.
18. Verify failure triggers rollback:
    - if backups were created, restore them,
    - remove broken newly written output,
    - return `VerifyFailed`.

## Logging Assertions

Use `tracing-test` (or equivalent) to capture logs in tests.
- Assert key trace messages appear: source resolved, write started, verify passed/failed, rollback actions.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Pipeline tests cover all branches and are deterministic.
- Logs emitted by pipeline are asserted for key stages.
- Verify-failure rollback invariants are asserted (no leftover "new" output when failing).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:07:17.545739660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T16:04:28.444272192Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T16:04:28.444251474Z","close_reason":"Implemented mock-provider pipeline suite with 15 deterministic tests and added pipeline same-provider shortcut, target-detect warning, and verify-failure rollback + VerifyFailed behavior.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["pipeline","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24z.9","depends_on_id":"bd-24z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:11.044946233Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24z.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:07:57.759639003Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24z.9","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:07:21.924294875Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24z.9","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:07:22.004626263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-264","title":"Implement Vibe provider adapter (simple JSONL with flexible field extraction)","description":"PORT Vibe provider from CASS: /dp/coding_agent_session_search/src/connectors/vibe.rs

WHAT IS VIBE: A Mistral-based coding assistant that stores session logs as JSONL.

SESSION FORMAT:
  Location: ~/.vibe/logs/session/{session-id}/messages.jsonl
  Env override: VIBE_HOME
  Directory structure: Each session gets its own subdirectory named by session ID.
  Each JSONL line: {"role":"user|assistant|system","content":"...","timestamp":"..."}

KEY DISTINGUISHING FEATURE — FLEXIBLE FIELD EXTRACTION:
  Vibe's JSONL isn't perfectly consistent. CASS handles this with fallback chains:
  - Role: Try "role" field → fallback to "speaker" → fallback to nested "message.role"
  - Content: Try "content" → fallback to "text" → fallback to nested "message.content"
  - Timestamp: Try "timestamp" → "created_at" → "createdAt" → "time" → "ts"
  This flexibility is the main complexity. Implement it carefully.

PROVIDER TRAIT IMPLEMENTATION:
  - slug: "vibe"
  - cli_alias: "vib"
  - name: "Vibe"
  - detect(): Check for ~/.vibe/logs/session/ directory (or VIBE_HOME)
  - session_roots(): Return [~/.vibe/logs/session/]
  - owns_session(id): Look for {id}/messages.jsonl or {id}/ directory in session roots
  - read_session(path): Parse messages.jsonl with flexible field extraction (role/content/timestamp fallbacks)
  - write_session(session, opts): Generate {session-id}/messages.jsonl with canonical field names. Use atomic_write.
  - resume_command(id): "vibe --resume {id}" (best guess)

CONSIDERATIONS:
  - Session ID comes from parent directory name, not filename
  - No workspace tracking (set workspace=None)
  - Title: first user message via truncate_title()
  - The flexible field extraction is the main value-add vs ClawdBot
  - No model tracking, no tool calls, no content blocks
  - Write path must create the session subdirectory

REFERENCE: CASS connector is ~130 lines. Follow ClawdBot pattern (once implemented) but add field fallbacks.

FILES TO CREATE/MODIFY:
  - CREATE: src/providers/vibe.rs
  - MODIFY: src/providers/mod.rs (add pub mod vibe)
  - MODIFY: src/discovery.rs (add to default_registry)
  - MODIFY: src/main.rs (update provider list in docs)
  - MODIFY: tests/json_contract_test.rs (update count + alias + VIBE_HOME env)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:05:28.388306288Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T03:11:21.370813213Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-2d7","title":"Add cross-provider test suite for all 14 providers (roundtrip, scalability, golden output)","description":"## Overview

After all 14 providers are implemented and wired, add comprehensive cross-provider tests that validate the full pipeline (read → canonical → write) works correctly for every provider combination.

This bead covers test infrastructure that spans multiple providers, NOT the per-provider unit tests (which are part of each provider's individual bead).

## Test Categories

### 1. Roundtrip Tests (`tests/roundtrip_test.rs`)
- For each provider: create synthetic native session → read → canonical → write → read again → compare
- Verify message count, roles, content, timestamps survive the roundtrip
- Test all 14 providers (currently tests exist for ~9)

### 2. Cross-Provider Pipeline Tests
- For each (source, target) pair where source != target:
  - Read source session → canonical → write to target → verify target file is valid
- Focus on the most common pairs: claude-code↔codex, gemini↔claude-code, etc.
- At minimum, test every new provider as both source and target against claude-code

### 3. Golden Output Tests (`tests/golden_output_test.rs`)
- For each new provider, add a golden input file under `tests/fixtures/` and a golden canonical output
- Verify that `read_session(golden_input)` produces exactly the golden canonical output
- This catches regressions in the reader logic

### 4. Scalability Tests (`tests/scalability_test.rs`)
- Verify that reading/writing large sessions (1000+ messages) works without excessive memory or time
- Test with at least one JSONL-based and one JSON-based provider

### 5. Discovery Tests (`tests/discovery_test.rs`)
- Test that `infer_provider_for_path()` correctly identifies all 14 providers
- Test ambiguous cases (e.g., JSONL files that could be multiple providers)
- Test the full `resolve_session()` flow for each new provider

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Roundtrip tests cover all 14 providers
- [ ] At least 5 cross-provider pipeline tests for new providers
- [ ] Golden output tests for all 5 new providers
- [ ] All tests pass, no regressions
- [ ] Total test count increases by 30+ from these new tests","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:09:54.326659226Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T03:11:35.163131085Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2d7","depends_on_id":"bd-352","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:11:35.163087383Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2rl","title":"EPIC: Canonical Session Model","description":"Define the canonical intermediate representation (IR) for coding agent sessions. This is the Rosetta Stone of casr: every provider's native format is normalized into this IR, and every target format is generated from it.

Port/adapt the conversion-relevant data types and helper logic from CASS (coding_agent_session_search), but keep the IR focused on *session conversion*, not CASS's indexing/search features.

The canonical model must capture:
- ordered messages with roles (user/assistant/tool/system)
- timestamps (normalized to epoch millis)
- workspace/cwd when available
- session identity (source id and generated target id)
- model name (session-level convenience + message-level author)
- tool calls/results when representable
- provider-specific passthrough fields (lossless preservation via `session.metadata` and `message.extra`)

Intentional non-goals (do NOT pull in CASS-only indexing baggage):
- snippet extraction/indexing types (casr can derive summaries from message content without storing snippets as first-class IR fields)
- database ids / remote-origin fields

## Success Criteria

- `CanonicalSession`/`CanonicalMessage` are expressive enough to write plausible sessions for core providers.
- Provider-specific fields that cannot be canonically represented are preserved in `metadata`/`extra`.
- Timestamp and content normalization helpers are centralized and exhaustively unit-tested.
- IR choices are documented so writer implementations have clear mapping rules.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:36:44.447987709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:41:43.484209368Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:41:43.484180534Z","close_reason":"All 3 children completed: CanonicalSession/CanonicalMessage types defined (bd-2rl.1), Provider trait defined (bd-2rl.2), all 5 content normalization helpers implemented (bd-2rl.3). The canonical IR is complete and ready for readers/writers to use.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","model"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2rl","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:04.457331048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2rl.1","title":"Define CanonicalSession and CanonicalMessage types","description":"Port and adapt the core session data model from CASS (conversion-relevant subset). Define these types in `src/model.rs`.

## Types

`CanonicalSession`:
- `session_id: String`
- `provider_slug: String` (source provider slug: `claude-code`, `codex`, `gemini`)
- `workspace: Option<PathBuf>` (project root / cwd)
- `title: Option<String>`
- `started_at: Option<i64>` (epoch millis)
- `ended_at: Option<i64>` (epoch millis)
- `messages: Vec<CanonicalMessage>`
- `metadata: serde_json::Value` (provider-specific extras that don't map to canonical fields; default `{}`)
- `source_path: Option<PathBuf>` (native file location when known)
- `model_name: Option<String>` (session-level convenience; may be derived from per-message author/model)

`CanonicalMessage`:
- `idx: usize` (0-based sequential order)
- `role: MessageRole`
- `content: String` (primary human-readable text)
- `timestamp: Option<i64>` (epoch millis)
- `author: Option<String>` (model name or identifier)
- `tool_calls: Vec<ToolCall>`
- `tool_results: Vec<ToolResult>`
- `extra: serde_json::Value` (provider-specific raw fields; default `{}`; SHOULD include raw native record/content blocks when available)

`MessageRole` enum:
- `User`
- `Assistant`
- `Tool`
- `System`
- `Other(String)`

Naming note:
- CASS uses "Agent" in some places; casr uses `Assistant` as the canonical variant. Normalization helpers should map `agent`/`model` -> `Assistant`.

Tool structs:
- `ToolCall { id: Option<String>, name: String, arguments: serde_json::Value }`
- `ToolResult { call_id: Option<String>, content: String, is_error: bool }`

Derives:
- All types derive `Debug`, `Clone`, `Serialize`, `Deserialize`.
- `CanonicalSession` and `CanonicalMessage` derive `PartialEq` for testing.

## Intentional Omissions vs CASS

Do NOT pull in CASS indexing/search-only fields (e.g., Snippet extraction/index ids). casr only needs conversion fidelity; summaries can be derived from message content.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Types compile cleanly with serde derives and `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`.
- Defaults for `metadata`/`extra` are stable (empty object, not null) to simplify writers.
- Canonical model supports representing tool calls/results without forcing provider-specific schemas.
- Unit tests validate serde round-trip and equality semantics for representative sessions/messages.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:39:20.241612244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:39:33.728375004Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:39:33.728355216Z","close_reason":"All types defined in src/model.rs: CanonicalSession (10 fields), CanonicalMessage (8 fields), MessageRole (5 variants), ToolCall, ToolResult. All derive Debug+Clone+Serialize+Deserialize, Session/Message also PartialEq. CASS naming note (Agent→Assistant) and deliberately omitted fields documented in module doc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","model"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2rl.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:10.438561462Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2rl.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:24.727876844Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
